description: Student applies rectangle properties (right angles, equal diagonals, length×breadth area) to all quadrilaterals, unable to differentiate between parallelogram, rhombus, rectangle, and square.
subject: quadrilaterals
diagnosis: Student's entire mental model of quadrilaterals is built around the rectangle. Cannot work with non-rectangular shapes correctly. This is a pervasive issue that blocks progress in all quadrilateral and area topics.
symptoms: Assumes all angles in a parallelogram are 90°
Uses length × breadth formula for all quadrilaterals
Cannot identify rhombus or trapezium properties
intervention_focus: Hands-on construction of non-rectangular parallelograms and rhombuses before any formula work

description: Student knows individual properties but cannot reliably match them to the correct quadrilateral — attributes rhombus properties to rectangles, parallelogram properties to trapeziums, etc.
subject: quadrilaterals
diagnosis: Properties have been memorized as isolated facts rather than tied to shape definitions. Student cannot reconstruct which property belongs to which shape under test conditions. Needs systematic property-shape mapping.
symptoms: Knows that 'diagonals bisect each other' but applies it to wrong shapes
Gives correct property list but for the wrong shape
Inconsistent across problems — gets some right, others wrong based on order of teaching
intervention_focus: Property table: systematically list Yes/No/Sometimes for each property across all quadrilateral types

description: Student confuses supplementary adjacent angles (180°) with equal opposite angles in parallelograms and trapeziums, applying the wrong rule to the wrong pair.
subject: quadrilaterals
diagnosis: Student has angle rules but applies them without considering which pair of angles the rule applies to. Needs explicit practice distinguishing 'opposite angles' from 'adjacent angles' in parallelograms and trapeziums.
symptoms: Sets adjacent angles equal instead of supplementary
In trapezium, says co-interior angles are equal
In equations, uses wrong sum (180° vs 360°) for adjacent vs all angles
intervention_focus: Diagrams showing same-side vs opposite angle pairs with color-coding for equal vs supplementary

description: Student applies the wrong area formula across quadrilateral types — most commonly uses the triangle formula (½ × b × h) for parallelograms or confuses area with perimeter.
subject: quadrilaterals
diagnosis: Area formulas for different shapes are not clearly differentiated. Student does not have a reliable procedure for selecting the correct formula based on the shape. Needs explicit formula discrimination practice.
symptoms: Parallelogram area is consistently half the correct value
Rhombus area is double the correct value (forgets ÷2 for diagonal formula)
Occasionally uses sum of sides when area is needed
intervention_focus: Side-by-side comparison: derive each area formula from first principles before practicing
